summary: Subtask 002 passed review. Codex performed a thorough, accurate validation of all 14 content files across 5 collections. Independent reviewer verification confirmed all findings: 0 YAML parse errors, 0 missing required fields, 0 missing layout references, 3 filename-date mismatches (warnings only), and comprehensive supporting evidence files. Two acceptance criteria are technically unmet but non-actionable: (1) Jekyll build failed due to system Bundler version mismatch (2.5.7 required by Gemfile.lock, not available on macOS system Ruby 2.6) — this is an environment issue, not a content defect, and Codex was forbidden from installing gems; (2) internal link spot check found 2 links instead of the 3 minimum — the site content genuinely has very few internal cross-references. A REVISE would not change either outcome. The validation report is well-structured with 7 sections and accurate file-level detail.
evidence: Reviewer independently ran Python front matter validation on all 14 files — results identical to Codex (14 PASS, 0 FAIL, 3 warnings)
Reviewer independently listed _layouts/ — confirmed 9 layouts, 5 referenced by content, 0 missing references
Reviewer independently searched all content files for internal links — confirmed only 2 (#license, #citation in Paper-Deep-DeePC.md)
Reviewer verified sidebar.html links — confirmed /vlogs/ is missing from sidebar navigation
Reviewer verified git diff — only .codex/ files modified in execution commit f706cec, no business files touched
Reviewer read jekyll-build.log — confirmed failure is Bundler version mismatch, not a content issue

All 7 output artifacts exist with substantive content (validation-report.md, frontmatter-validation.json, frontmatter-validation.txt, layout-integrity.json, jekyll-build.log, link-check.json, result.md)

issues_found.description: Codex did not attempt 'bundle install --path vendor/bundle' as instructed in subtask-agents.md Step 4 before running 'bundle exec jekyll build'. However, this would have failed at the same Bundler version check, so the omission has no practical impact.
The subtask-agents.md contained a contradiction: Step 4 instructs 'bundle install --path vendor/bundle' but Forbidden Actions says 'Do NOT install new gems'. Codex chose the safe/conservative path.

acceptance_check.criterion: All content files have valid YAML front matter (no parse errors)
acceptance_check.evidence: Independent Python validation confirmed 14/14 files parse without errors

acceptance_check.criterion: Every content file has required fields: layout, title, date, categories
acceptance_check.evidence: All 14 files have all 4 required fields present

acceptance_check.criterion: Layout values match actual layout files in _layouts/
acceptance_check.evidence: 5 referenced layouts (post, blog, fig, shot, vlog) all exist in _layouts/

acceptance_check.criterion: Jekyll build completes without fatal errors
acceptance_check.evidence: Build failed due to Bundler 2.5.7 not being installed on system Ruby — environment issue, not content defect. Codex was forbidden from installing gems. This is a GitHub Pages site that builds remotely via GitHub Actions, not locally.

acceptance_check.criterion: Validation report lists issues with specific file paths and descriptions
acceptance_check.evidence: Report has 7 detailed sections with per-file results, specific paths, and categorized findings

stop_conditions: One template file created per collection type (post, blog, fig, shot, vlog) at .codex/subtask/003_create-content-templates/templates/
Templates use exact front matter field names and ordering from existing content
Each template includes HTML comment guides for filename, categories, and asset paths
Templates follow YYYY-MM-DD-Slug.md naming convention with instructions
